overview.tagline: A friendly hilltop borough with a vibrant downtown, strong schools, and unbeatable convenience
overview.description: The Borough of Wood-Ridge is a compact, welcoming community situated on a prominent ridge in southern Bergen County. Despite its small size, Wood-Ridge punches above its weight with a lively downtown along Valley Boulevard and Hackensack Street, well-regarded schools, and a proud community spirit. The borough's elevated position offers scenic views, and its location near major highways, retail corridors, and the Meadowlands makes it exceptionally convenient for commuters and families alike.
highlights: Charming downtown along Valley Boulevard with local restaurants and shops
Elevated hilltop location with views of the Manhattan skyline
Strong K-12 school district with small-town attention
Excellent highway access via Route 17 and Route 46
Active community events and strong civic pride

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Wood-Ridge's housing stock includes a charming mix of Cape Cods, Colonials, split-levels, and newer construction.
realEstate.recentTrends: Wood-Ridge has experienced strong price appreciation, driven by its affordability relative to neighboring towns, convenient location, and walkable downtown. First-time buyers and young families are particularly active in the market. Updated homes and new construction command premium prices.
neighborhoodNotes: Valley Boulevard area — Walking distance to downtown shops and restaurants
Highland Avenue section — Elevated homes with some Manhattan skyline views
Hackensack Street area — Mix of residential and commercial along the main route
Wood-Ridge Heights — Homes on the higher elevation with larger lots
Route 17 corridor area — Newer construction and townhomes near retail

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment through DPW
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly for all residential properties.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on recycling day
Yard waste collected seasonally
Recycling bins available from the borough
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer services are provided by the Borough of Wood-Ridge.
details: Municipal water system operated by the Borough
Sewer service maintained by Borough DPW
Quarterly billing for water and sewer
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency water repairs available 24/7

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages parking in the downtown area and residential zones.
details: Street parking available in the downtown area with time limits
Municipal parking lot near Valley Boulevard
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Free parking available in some commercial areas

additionalServices.title: Wood-Ridge Memorial Library
additionalServices.description: Community library offering books, digital resources, and programs.
details: Located at 231 Hackensack Street
Children's programs, teen events, and adult book clubs
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Year-round recreational programs for residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues including baseball, soccer, and basketball
Adult programs and senior activities
Summer camp and after-school programs
Community events and holiday celebrations

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Wood-Ridge enforces noise regulations to maintain residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM only
No construction noise permitted on Sundays or federal holidays
Loud music and amplified sound restricted after 10:00 PM
Leaf blowers and power equipment restricted to daytime hours

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained and grass kept at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours after snowfall
Fences must comply with height regulations
Abandoned vehicles must be removed from property
Property exteriors must be maintained in good repair

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Wood-Ridge requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with proof of rabies vaccination
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Excessive barking subject to complaints and fines
No exotic or wild animals permitted as pets

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking is regulated on borough streets for safety and access.
keyRules: No overnight parking on borough streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Time limits in the downtown business district
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles prohibited from residential streets overnight
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Inspections required at change of tenancy
Landlords must maintain properties to housing code standards
Fire safety requirements for multi-family units
Certificate of Occupancy required for rental units

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Garage and yard sales are permitted with restrictions.
keyRules: Permit required from the Borough Clerk
Limited number of sales per year per household
Sales restricted to designated hours
Signs must be removed promptly after the sale

publicSchools.name: Wood-Ridge High School
publicSchools.notes: Combined junior and senior high school known for strong academics, athletics, and community involvement

publicSchools.name: Catherine E. Doyle Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Primary school serving all Wood-Ridge students in grades K-3

publicSchools.name: Robert L. Craig Intermediate School
publicSchools.notes: Intermediate school serving Wood-Ridge students in grades 4-6

privateSchools.name: Assumption School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic parish school serving the Wood-Ridge community

whyMoveHere: Walkable downtown with local restaurants and shops
Affordable by Bergen County standards with strong community
Excellent highway access via Route 17, Route 46, and Route 3
Manhattan skyline views from elevated sections of the borough
K-12 school district with small-town feel
Active community events and civic organizations

commuteToNYC.duration: 30-50 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: Wood-Ridge residents commute to NYC primarily via NJ Transit bus service along Hackensack Street and Valley Boulevard with routes to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. The commute is approximately 30-50 minutes depending on traffic. The nearby Wesmont station area and connections at Secaucus Junction provide additional options.

costOfLiving.summary: Wood-Ridge offers an affordable entry point to Bergen County living, with home prices below the county median and a compact community that minimizes daily expenses.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 20% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Affordable homes by Bergen County standards
Charming downtown with restaurants and local businesses
Strong community spirit with active civic organizations
cons: No NJ Transit rail station within the borough
Compact borough with smaller lot sizes
Proximity to Teterboro Airport means occasional aircraft noise
Property taxes remain significant by national standards
Limited parkland due to small borough size

restaurants.name: Vesta Wood Fired Pizza & Bar
restaurants.description: Popular restaurant featuring artisan wood-fired pizzas, creative cocktails, and a modern atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Kiku Japanese Restaurant
restaurants.description: Japanese restaurant offering sushi, sashimi, hibachi, and traditional Japanese dishes.

restaurants.name: Maria's Restaurant
restaurants.description: Family-owned Italian restaurant serving homestyle pasta, seafood, and classic Italian-American dishes.

restaurants.name: Wood-Ridge Diner
restaurants.description: Classic New Jersey diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with a comprehensive American menu.

parks.name: Veterans Memorial Park
parks.description: Borough's main park featuring sports fields, playground equipment, basketball courts, and a memorial honoring local veterans.

parks.name: Bianchi Field
parks.description: Sports complex with baseball and softball fields, used for youth leagues and community events.

parks.name: Borough Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with playground, picnic area, and open green space.

events.name: Wood-Ridge Street Fair
events.description: Annual street fair along Valley Boulevard featuring food vendors, crafts, live entertainment, and family activities.

events.name: Memorial Day Parade
events.description: Annual parade through the borough honoring veterans and fallen service members.
events.when: Memorial Day, last Monday in May

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting & Festivities
events.description: Community holiday celebration with tree lighting, caroling, refreshments, and a visit from Santa.

attractions.name: Valley Boulevard Downtown
attractions.description: Wood-Ridge's main street featuring local restaurants, pizzerias, bakeries, and shops in a walkable setting.

attractions.name: Manhattan Skyline Views
attractions.description: The elevated sections of Wood-Ridge, particularly along Highland Avenue, offer scenic views of the Manhattan skyline, especially striking at sunset.

shoppingAreas: Valley Boulevard — Downtown restaurants and local shops
Hackensack Street — Additional businesses and services
Garden State Plaza (Paramus) — Major mall a short drive north

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 1st and 3rd Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 85 Humboldt Street, Wood-Ridge, NJ 07075

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
